input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Accumulator`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Accumulator` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.1.3
            TITLE: accumulator

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.1.3: Accumulator
EV.1.3 Accumulator  
All the battery cells or super capacitors that store the electrical energy to be used by the 
Tractive System

RULE: f.10.5.7
            TITLE: accumulator attachment – load based

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.5.7: Accumulator Attachment – Load Based
F.10.5.7 Accumulator Attachment – Load Based  
a. The minimum number of attachment points depends on the total mass of the container: 
  Accumulator Weight Minimum Attachment Points  
  < 20 kg   4  
  20 – 30 kg  6  
  30 – 40 kg  8  
  > 40 kg   10  
b. Each attachment point, including any brackets, backing plates and inserts, must be able 
to withstand 15 kN minimum in any direction
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 52 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: ev.5.2.2
            TITLE: if the accumulator container is made from an electrically conductive material:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.2.2: If the Accumulator Container is made from an electrically conductive material:
EV.5.2.2 If the Accumulator Container is made from an electrically conductive material: 
a. The poles of the Accumulator Segment(s) and/or cells must be isolated from the inner 
wall of the Accumulator Container with an insulating material that is rated for the 
maximum Tractive System voltage.    
b. All conductive surfaces on the outside of the Accumulator Container must have a low 
resistance connection to the GLV System Ground, see EV.6.7 
c. Any conductive penetrations, such as mounting hardware, must be protected against 
puncturing the insulating barrier.

RULE: ev.7.3.1
            TITLE: an accumulator management system must monitor the accumulator(s) voltage ev.7.4 and

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.3.1: An Accumulator Management System must monitor the Accumulator(s) Voltage EV.7.4 and
EV.7.3.1 An Accumulator Management System must monitor the Accumulator(s) Voltage EV.7.4 and 
Temperature EV.7.5 when the:  
a. Tractive System is Active  EV.11.5 
b. Accumulator is connected to a Charger  EV.8.3

RULE: ev.10.1.1
            TITLE: the accumulator must be onsite at the time the team registers to be eligible for accumulator

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.10.1.1: The Accumulator must be onsite at the time the team registers to be eligible for Accumulator
EV.10.1.1 The Accumulator must be onsite at the time the team registers to be eligible for Accumulator 
Technical Inspection and Dynamic Events

RULE: ev.5.4.1
            TITLE: every accumulator container must contain minimum one fuse (ev.6.6) and two or more

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.4.1: Every Accumulator Container must contain minimum one fuse (EV.6.6) and two or more
EV.5.4.1 Every Accumulator Container must contain minimum one fuse (EV.6.6) and two or more 
Accumulator Isolation Relays (AIR)

RULE: ev.5.6.1
            TITLE: the accumulator must contain a precharge circuit.  the precharge circuit must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.6.1: The Accumulator must contain a Precharge Circuit.  The Precharge Circuit must:
EV.5.6.1 The Accumulator must contain a Precharge Circuit.  The Precharge Circuit must: 
a. Be able to charge the Intermediate Circuit to minimum 90% of the Accumulator voltage 
before closing the second AIR 
b. Be supplied from the Shutdown Circuit  EV.7.1 
c. Not be fused

RULE: ev.11.5.1
            TITLE: accumulators must be removed from the vehicle inside the accumulator container and placed

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.11.5.1: Accumulators must be removed from the vehicle inside the Accumulator Container and placed
EV.11.5.1 Accumulators must be removed from the vehicle inside the Accumulator Container and placed 
on the Accumulator Container Hand Cart EV.4.10 for Charging.

RULE: f.10.5.3
            TITLE: any fasteners used to attach accumulator container(s) are critical fasteners, see t.8.2

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.5.3: Any fasteners used to attach Accumulator Container(s) are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2
F.10.5.3 Any fasteners used to attach Accumulator Container(s) are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: ev.5.3.3
            TITLE: when the accumulator containers are opened or segments are removed, the accumulator

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.3.3: When the Accumulator Containers are opened or Segments are removed, the Accumulator
EV.5.3.3 When the Accumulator Containers are opened or Segments are removed, the Accumulator 
Segments must be separated by using the Maintenance Plugs.  See EV.11.4.1

RULE: f.11.1.1
            TITLE: all accumulator containers must lie inside the primary structure (f.1.10).

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.1.1: All Accumulator Containers must lie inside the Primary Structure (F.1.10).
F.11.1.1 All Accumulator Containers must lie inside the Primary Structure (F.1.10).

RULE: f.10.4.1
            TITLE: the accumulator container(s) exterior or interior walls may contain holes or openings, see

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.4.1: The Accumulator Container(s) exterior or interior walls may contain holes or openings, see
F.10.4.1 The Accumulator Container(s) exterior or interior walls may contain holes or openings, see

RULE: ev.5.1.1
            TITLE: all cells or super capacitors which store the tractive system energy are built into accumulator

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.1.1: All cells or super capacitors which store the Tractive System energy are built into Accumulator
EV.5.1.1 All cells or super capacitors which store the Tractive System energy are built into Accumulator 
Segments and must be enclosed in (an) Accumulator Container(s).

RULE: ev.11.5.2
            TITLE: accumulator charging must occur only inside the designated area

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.11.5.2: Accumulator Charging must occur only inside the designated area
EV.11.5.2 Accumulator Charging must occur only inside the designated area

RULE: ev.7.5.1
            TITLE: the ams must measure the temperatures of critical points of the accumulator

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.5.1: The AMS must measure the temperatures of critical points of the Accumulator
EV.7.5.1 The AMS must measure the temperatures of critical points of the Accumulator

RULE: f.10.1.3
            TITLE: the accumulator containers and mounting systems are subject to approval during ses review

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.1.3: The Accumulator Containers and mounting systems are subject to approval during SES review
F.10.1.3 The Accumulator Containers and mounting systems are subject to approval during SES review 
and Technical Inspection

RULE: f.10.1.1
            TITLE: all accumulator containers must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.1.1: All Accumulator Containers must be:
F.10.1.1 All Accumulator Containers must be:  
a. Designed to withstand forces from deceleration in all directions 
b. Made from a Nonflammable Material ( F.1.18 )

RULE: f.10.5.1
            TITLE: attachment of the accumulator container must be documented in the ses

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.5.1: Attachment of the Accumulator Container must be documented in the SES
F.10.5.1 Attachment of the Accumulator Container must be documented in the SES

RULE: t.1.8.5
            TITLE: (ev only) the accumulator container must not be part of the firewall

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.5: (EV only) The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Firewall
T.1.8.5 (EV only) The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Firewall

RULE: f.10.5.6
            TITLE: accumulator attachment – corner attachments

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.5.6: Accumulator Attachment – Corner Attachments
F.10.5.6 Accumulator Attachment – Corner Attachments 
a. Eight or more attachments are required for any configuration.   
• One attachment for each corner of a rectangular structure of multiple Accumulator 
Segments 
• More than the minimum number of fasteners may be required for non rectangular 
arrangements 
Examples: If not filled in with additional structure, an extruded L shape would require 
attachments at 10 convex corners (the corners at the inside of the L are not convex); 
an extruded hexagon would require 12 attachments 
b. The mechanical connections at each corner must be 50 mm or less from the corner of 
the Segment 
c. Each attachment point must be able to withstand a Test Load equal to 1/4 of total mass 
of the container accelerating at 40 g
